title: TFIM gap via exact diagonalization
summary: The transverse-field Ising model (TFIM) energy gap between ground and first excited state, computed by full diagonalization of the finite-chain Hamiltonian. Serves as the primary benchmark observable for the toy-model validation lane.
assumptions: The chain is finite with open or periodic boundary conditions.
The transverse field parameter h is real and bounded away from the critical point for convergence tests.

boundary_conditions: Hamiltonian matrix is constructed and diagonalized exactly
Gap = E_1 - E_0 from sorted eigenvalues

applies_to: toy-model lane validation: gap reproduction against known analytic or numerical baselines

promotion.rationale: Seed unit for toy-model lane: TFIM gap as the canonical exact-diagonalization benchmark observable.

payload.definition: For a TFIM chain of N sites with transverse field h, the gap is Delta(h,N) = E_1 - E_0 where E_0, E_1 are the two lowest eigenvalues of the 2^N x 2^N Hamiltonian matrix H = -J sum sigma_z^i sigma_z^{i+1} - h sum sigma_x^i.
payload.operational_role: Primary benchmark observable for toy-model lane: the gap must be reproduced within specified tolerance by any ED implementation.
